title: Publisher Confirms, Windows, Failure Ambiguity, and Republish · Boundary Failure Triage
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but defective confirm-window and ambiguous-publication ledger; preserve message identity and timing, locate the first broken ownership boundary, and retain a deterministic regression case. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in confirm-window and ambiguous-publication ledger and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evi…
objectives: Use confirm channels and account for callback results, waitForConfirms, nack, return ordering, close, and unresolved publication state.
Bound unconfirmed count, bytes, and age while preserving throughput, memory, fairness, and per-message outcome evidence.
Explain when confirms occur for unroutable messages, persistent classic queues, quorum queues, and multiple routed queues.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Confirm Channel Contract
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug messaging team handles case 4069 in a privacy-sensitive audit fanout with an offline subscriber. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound the confirm window and preserve ambiguity when a channel closes before proof; retain a screen-reader-readable delivery and ownership trace; then change one routing segment and prove the exact destination set. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use confirm channels and account for callback results, waitForConfirms, nack, return ordering, close, and unresolved publication state.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Publish the incident record · Debug Publisher Ledger Cleanup
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Publisher Ledger Cleanup: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A debug messaging team handles case 8021 in a privacy-sensitive audit fanout with an offline subscriber. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound the confirm window and preserve ambiguity when a channel closes before proof; retain a screen-reader-readable delivery and ownership trace; then raise a broker resource alarm and stop new publisher admission. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Retire confirmed entries, reject nacked entries, preserve ambiguous entries, cancel waiters, and prove zero memory or callback leaks across channel generations.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Publish the incident record · Debug Publisher Ledger Cleanup.
steps.why: Retrieving Publisher Ledger Cleanup through the concrete confirm-window and ambiguous-publication ledger case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Publish the incident record · Debug Publisher Ledger Cleanup, keep Publisher Ledger Cleanup connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Retire confirmed entries, reject nacked entries, preserve ambiguous entries, cancel waiters, and prove zero memory or callback leaks across channel generations.

hints: For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Publish the incident record · Debug Publisher Ledger Cleanup, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Publisher Ledger Cleanup precondition, or invariant that must hold.

Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Retire confirmed entries, reject nacked entries, preserve ambiguous entries, cancel waiters, and prove zero memory or callback leaks across channel generations.

For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Publish the incident record · Debug Publisher Ledger Cleanup, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: The client library automatically retains a durable application ledger across reconnection.
